supply_chain.dependencies: The Company depends on third-party suppliers for products and services essential to operations. Licensing operations depend on third-party manufacturers, game developers, publishers and retailers for merchandise production and distribution. Theme parks and resorts depend on third-party suppliers for goods and services.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions mentioned as potential impact on operations. Consolidation and market conditions in cable/satellite/telecommunications distribution industry may adversely affect programming distribution terms. Competition for creative talent, sports programming rights, and production facilities. Dependence on third-party licensees and sellers for merchandise licensing revenues. Significant contraction of distribution channels could adversely impact profitability.

regulatory_risks: FCC regulation of television and radio networks, programming networks and owned television stations including licensing requirements, ownership limits, dual network restrictions, foreign ownership restrictions, and programming content regulation

Privacy and data protection laws including GDPR (EU/UK), state-level comprehensive data privacy laws (California and other U.S. states), children's online privacy laws (COPPA, UK Age Appropriate Design Code)
Data breach notification requirements in U.S. states and international jurisdictions
International content regulations requiring minimum local content production, investment in local content, or content produced by local independent production companies (EU, Canada)
Content regulation laws impacting ability to distribute certain films and programming in specific jurisdictions
Safety and supply chain regulations for consumer products and theme park operations
Land planning, use and development regulations for theme parks
U.S. and international anti-corruption laws, sanction programs, trade restrictions, anti-money laundering laws, currency controls
Domestic and international labor laws, tax laws and antitrust laws
Potential new regulations limiting international trade and investment
Uncertain legal and regulatory landscape for new technologies including AI and streaming services
State government legislation targeted at specific sectors and companies
Novel applications of existing laws to new technologies

sanction_risks: U.S. and other countries implemented sanctions against Russia in response to events in Russia and Ukraine in 2022
U.S. agencies have enhanced trade restrictions including new prohibitions on importation of goods from certain regions
Company paused certain operations in certain regions in response to sanctions, trade restrictions and related developments
Profitability of certain operations impacted by events in corresponding regions
Ongoing developments in international political, trade and security policy may lead to new regulations limiting international trade and investment
Economic or political conditions in certain countries have reduced and could continue to reduce ability to hedge currency exposure or repatriate revenue
